textByHash.04f5baacb4b4a34b8a187c167171e1552480625c: In that case you should tell this to your cat, 'No guts, no glory!'
textByHash.0810ecfca926d919aa953b04a66f0a290bcbeab2: I did indeed! A performance for the ages.
textByHash.0d36498a2ee491f7cf15547773f12d2ae543fadc: In that case you should tell your cat: 'Stay in for as long as you can.'
textByHash.1958f559ce1b37a85e87406834d9bf71553d3899: Indeed, word has gotten to me from our other ratcatcher chapters that you are quite a talent.
textByHash.1c721dc3a5e6e5f940e82688c72ad6bdf8e6f78d: In that case you should tell your cat, 'Go berserk!'
textByHash.1c8325ea7fc1aa622a5cb42b73f05c1b8efa0b96: No, silly. A poem about the art of ratcatching.
textByHash.1ca87fd1b8ddc0bc45369a890d5e7f6ba7ad9cb2: I'll only help you name wily or lazy cats. Bring me one and I'll help you out.
textByHash.20793bda83033ec1789c0968d0b78d7423a463f1: Wily cats need looking after or else they get fat and lazy.
textByHash.23a77e21320e783b4a1b144e63eae9c51e2db97c: Yep. I expect to see all the rats in Port Sarim dead in one, decisive action.
textByHash.26713287e0fb492f773416751d80c7fd53b7f6ff: Nothing too difficult, just rid the port of every single rat, with just one action.
textByHash.2795e1e2a26dd5a57e976ecf163eab0a839f94c5: The key is to keep it active, bring it hunting or to the ratpits regularly.
textByHash.28d8a994350c77f190c2f383ce58aabdb8d8daf3: Epic poems aren't usually commissioned by the shy and retiring.
textByHash.2ae07b3c956a125ac643948e2b3b5d7381bb24ec: I can only train overgrown cats into wily cats.
textByHash.2bc899ce9f0f26cf1a8d127836b374fc216786a5: I do indeed. In fact, that's why I wanted to talk to you.
textByHash.2d595ded7c9965f59fe362c32e6476149bc43cba: They would get fat and lazy, be much worse at hunting and would show you up as a really poor cat trainer.
textByHash.2e1c6e99bf10c457df2b81848d5c231081d5bb53: Bring it hunting or take it to the Rat Pits regularly.
textByHash.3023d08a64ad5c83ee7cc13524d3dfa7c7e3efd9: Don't be so hard on yourself!
textByHash.327139bc7ea6ba37d4a08ea42e2f06e1544e941f: It depends on what kind of cat you have.
textByHash.44b8830c921922a57914346a4f0344328cccc95d: Just as soon as I get around to writing it all down.
textByHash.48f539699f1dc06d0225b8831dcedde16e2a00d4: I want to write a new poem.
textByHash.49ae9f0bef443492ed5ba6bcb87d647245a94d0f: Try keep it happy too, they can be a little nasty when they're cranky.
textByHash.4af0c5133f5d2d686c7d193b815f77b5fadf510b: You should wear an Amulet of Catspeak. It will allow you to organise tactics with your cat.
textByHash.4b287c199bf75873008e65f0dcd486cc01a688c2: Nothing too difficult. Just rid the port of every single rat... at once.
textByHash.4fcfec9a8aef8765a17faac0e6655483e20a7745: Wily cats need lots more exercise, otherwise they'll get fat and lazy.
textByHash.509d4f7e4d911d6e645771640a1485f16493c266: Well if that's the case then your are left with two choices.
textByHash.5848347c398ebafd4bf065e8cc3a17dd0a572011: You could go for all out attack which will boost your cat's attack and strength, but weaken it
textByHash.5b68e3b493c1129311596d7c7d5ff8b864238337: Oh yes? Has The Face been telling tales again?
textByHash.62bac88e4f09c6740dd7ca0c26cd025a092ca92e: In that case you should tell your cat, 'Just take care of yourself cat.'
textByHash.6303475a5cfefbf8ff2d779f901377ed3e491622: In the Port Sarim pits, your cat will become happier and fitter after fighting.
textByHash.6396484bdaf9c3d1b6a5ad207ade7e5438ce5143: No I need you to carry out just one task for me.
textByHash.6751bec4cf902051494227a8860b40f9d7f6de5f: Now that you're a real ratcatcher, I'd be happy to train that cat of yours.
textByHash.69fe39469b107423ed0590d8a679397b79cbafcb: Yes you can get your lazy cat back in shape with a lot of rat catching, but you really shouldn't let it get into that state in the first place.
textByHash.6bb88e2b159a7d052b12d7790eed78182add2484: Any luck killing all the rats yet?
textByHash.6c66c9b263518f091f00197bcf811a6ffa68ff7b: They're tougher, stronger and faster than other kinds of cats. They have superior hunting skills and can even catch butterflies and kalphite larvae.
textByHash.6c8067766cf84ac43cec170f54ca0edcd28a20be: Here take this, you might find it useful.
textByHash.70eb3f26e026ad14b970d9b23d1b6cd22b69091f: It's all about objectivity and integrity. I need to be utterly divorced from the process.
textByHash.713332eff28715f95826aacfe2f3044ee2d46d43: In that case you should tell your cat: 'No guts, no glory!'
textByHash.720043b3d23d48ad48b3633bf5635b995d643780: Well what do you want to know?
textByHash.722c19d91cd96e33e4b34312eddff9c4ba4b2f30: Poetry might be my bread and butter, but catching rats is my real passion.
textByHash.7802c36ba0e3a102af9aab70611a3539075491d9: Ah, ah, ah - not so fast.
textByHash.78b6b89d1b963d59c8a104437bfbdf7234dd5821: Or, go for a less aggressive stance, which will increase your cat's strength and attack to a lesser degree, but carries no defensive penalties.
textByHash.7a96913730577a55b240d9c5d8c4d15015bd3888: Your other option is to adopt a less defensive strategy which will allow your cat to stay in the pit for longer.
textByHash.7aab69505217da7966905c9b1aaa18e56283a4ff: Yes, you can get your lazy cat back in shape with lots of rat catching. But you really shouldn't let things get that bad.
textByHash.7ed1583101f6882bfe535eda9c9e6b705f965e0f: Well seagulls rarely make good central characters. They're a bit too ... uhm.... flighty.
textByHash.8026bf52b847bcd743269d7f98cf53c023091069: What do you want to know?
textByHash.8a5c24fed5bf50c1b1b5b13b2c62ac599d229580: Ah, modesty. A trait seldom displayed by adventurers like yourself.
textByHash.8ac89e4b76ed9ee77f2b5775c9c0911ae26b5a6c: Besides, it's not difficult. I myself performed the very same feat just a few years ago.
textByHash.91c09ad941dec0213be2321c73ec27181f79b9de: Rid the port of all its rats with one action.
textByHash.9ab6c1e2d6d63f8e06acac731893dfeb6d438280: Or you can adopt a less defensive strategy, which will allow your cat to stay in the pit for longer.
textByHash.9c040c41b9e76ed31ee7c483556936870e0c04dc: In that case you should tell your cat, 'Stay in for as long as you can.'
textByHash.b0cc142f160cd22cb8dcdf2265990ac78467cd9f: Ha! Nice try. You'll have to figure that out for yourself.
textByHash.b8b4da058a39643d73626f1ae05666b59358352e: In that case you should tell your cat: 'Go berserk!'
textByHash.bc0e6a0b5fa64de6565df0734ac22bb88fdc2921: You already have a mature cat, look after that first.
textByHash.be5b39c0cf0ec6b55f2a5840f21b136743c6d9d2: You can adopt an ultra defensive strategy, which increases your cat's hitpoints and defence, the drawback to this is that your cat will flee sooner.
textByHash.c2c436c9e3f5f016c2f0bfc9d66a1d2d252cfc87: Indeed I do. It's for this very reason that I requested to talk to you.
textByHash.c2ec5205e6079de9f52d2478e3814269c700fffa: I can only train overgrown cats into wilycats.
textByHash.c3fa18a52ec6e533038085925391ea057e95a734: You haven't completed the task yet.
textByHash.c821ed388bfcfb125ba44c0169b30115c2058e62: A heroic cat like yours needs a heroic name, don't you think?
textByHash.c88739adf3c8b6b7012b30cee6c8a24618271026: You can adopt an ultra defensive strategy, which increases your cat's hitpoints and defence but causes it to flee sooner.
textByHash.c92fff8bd74c589eaf9b76a3f3cd33197d537a3a: Try and keep it happy too, wily cats can be a little nasty when they're cranky.
textByHash.c9a4d7001c91a9ecd780a6aa3f9967d405967dfe: It'll get fat and lazy and become much worse at hunting. Plus, everyone will know you're a bad cat trainer.
textByHash.cd1bfe51bb68853b879970b373d618e6e89f8d09: Do you have any other questions about the pits?
textByHash.ce6ed4e39cccd9a015239eceac5cc53e9950566c: Epic poems of bravery and heroics aren't usually commissioned by the shy and retiring.
textByHash.d32deafe065fbda551f9ecc726179977af69c0d1: Has the face being telling tall tales again?
textByHash.d84d1cd020ed68157ec668e9fc2905d57d11d524: Too many more like you and I'll lose half of my business.
textByHash.da5aebadc58bb6d237b45e5ab0ea1507df081746: Well if that's the case then you're left with two choices.
textByHash.dd6e0842552f6c989c4241ce776bd712956f7877: Besides the poem is about me not you.
textByHash.e41bfea4c1720be75934a7edb7add327a0f5f3d2: In that case you are left with two choices.
textByHash.e4ef7fcbebfa78ced9957c88b3858c521ea651ed: Modesty, a trait seldom displayed by adventurers these days.
textByHash.ea6d4911e0d1e5fcd0ebb019cd1b2ddaf85136bc: Ah, we meet at last. My fellow ratcatchers tell me you're quite the talent.
textByHash.ed684f7d51c8ed0b144dace0c429aa3119b86139: Of course, of course, they're the highest form of cat that I know of.
textByHash.edb589b6476a3d77771cd763bdab0b91179629b7: Kill all the rats in Port Sarim in one, swift action.
textByHash.ee585f351f6902ad064abecb024be929c0ab7179: or go for a less aggressive stance which will increase your cat's strength and attack to a lesser degree, but carries no defensive penalties.
textByHash.f3667bb8c7374b1b14bf781bb745d74970e3068b: They're tougher, stronger and faster than other variations of cat, they can catch butterflies and kalphite larva and are far superior to any other type of cat at hunting.
textByHash.f56bcddfd9343c49ae5faf1b1a30ce23d0206abe: In that case you should tell your cat: 'Just take care of yourself, cat.'
textByHash.f77a3da49f3edcd28d9815b6bddf1482884d4307: You should really name your cat.
textByHash.f94f1a9d97f953f7c3e9f538da11bbabeb95214d: I myself carried out the very same feat once upon a time.
textByHash.fade9a4ca2d0c7aefe8078cee70fa0669622dce1: Well, that depends on your outlook, whether you're ready to risk your cat's life or not and how defensive or aggressive you want your cat to be.
textByHash.fcab10ba4a5090ba31825e6c8fe988e20ae79211: You could go for all out attack which will boost your cat's attack and strength, but weaken it.
textByHash.fec61f8cb3d9db249f1e60b32b2fc5f84ad36648: Ah yes, the poem... your performance was incredibly inspiring. I think it'll be a real masterpiece...
textByHash.ff510b8773e1f9edff0a01a3e67ec6404bc7c1c2: Here take this, you might find it useful
textByHash.ff5b3ef45b0c457cb127263e1c479b350734f06d: Yes I saw that, now what can I do for you? Don't keep me overly long, I'm trying to complete my poem.
